title: How Alcohol Affects Your Gut Microbiome
description: Long-form YouTube explainer (8–10 min) on the relationship between alcohol consumption and gut health: dysbiosis, intestinal permeability, downstream inflammation. Animated diagrams over a host narration.
hook: Your gut bacteria are begging you to read this.
keyMessage: Even moderate drinking measurably shifts your gut microbiome.

cta.text: Subscribe for the rest of the gut-health series

strategy.keyMessage: Even moderate drinking measurably shifts your gut microbiome.
strategy.ctaText: Subscribe for the rest of the gut-health series.
doChecklist: Use peer language, not lecture tone
dontChecklist: Don't overstate causality past what the studies show

hookBank: Your gut bacteria are begging you to read this.
One drink. Measurable change in your microbiome.
What alcohol does to your gut starts at the first sip.
outputs.ctaLine: Subscribe for the rest of the gut-health series.

videoLong.hook: Your gut bacteria are begging you to read this. Even moderate drinking — what your doctor would call 'within guidelines' — measurably reshapes your microbiome. In 9 minutes I'll walk you through what changes, why it matters, and what reverses.

sequenceBlocks.description: Cold open: animated petri dish; VO sets up the misalignment between 'moderate' guidelines and what the studies actually show.
Section 1: What the microbiome does (1-min refresher). Animated explainer of bacterial diversity, immune signaling, neurotransmitter production.
Section 2: The mechanism. Show 2024 study graph: bacterial-diversity drops measurable from one drink. Lower-third citation.
Section 3: Downstream effects. Inflammation, leaky gut, mood. Pace this slow — most viewers will be hearing this for the first time.
Section 4: What reverses. Timeline of recovery in studies of dry-30 participants.
Recap. Subscribe pitch tied to the next episode (sleep + gut crossover).

videoLong.voiceoverNotes: Authoritative but not preachy. Stress every citation. Avoid the word 'should' — let the data do the moralising.
